claims.claim_text: In software delivery, deploying changes incrementally to a small subset of traffic enables early detection of defects while keeping the old version available, provided the new and old versions are backward compatible.
claims.boundary_rationale: The anchor contains a single compound assertion linking incremental deployment to defect detection and version availability, contingent on backward compatibility. No sub-clauses can be independently evaluated without losing the core logical dependency.

claims.claim_text: When an incremental rollout with backward compatibility is used for a change, the first cohort will expose any critical regression without affecting the rest of the system.
claims.boundary_rationale: The text makes a specific forward-looking assertion about the behavior of the 'first cohort' under defined conditions. It is a single predictive unit.

claims.claim_text: A defect found in the first cohort does not require rolling back the old version; only the cohort is rolled back.
claims.boundary_rationale: This asserts a specific observable outcome (scope of rollback) resulting from a defect in the first cohort. It is a distinct prediction from the previous anchor.

claims.claim_text: The old version remains fully operational during the entire rollout phase.
claims.boundary_rationale: This defines a specific state (operational status of the old version) expected to hold true throughout a defined period, serving as a success criterion.

claims.claim_text: If an incremental rollout with backward compatibility still causes a system-wide outage or forces rollback of the old version, the pattern is falsified.
claims.boundary_rationale: The text explicitly defines the conditions under which the pattern would be considered false (system-wide outage or old version rollback).

claims.claim_text: The entire system becomes unavailable or the old version is rolled back due to issues from the incremental rollout.
claims.boundary_rationale: This excerpt describes the specific observable events that constitute the falsification condition mentioned in the related statement.
